steps.name: create-branch
steps.description: Create task/t0014_v2_annotator_sonnet_rerun branch from main and record base commit.

steps.name: check-deps
steps.description: Verify t0009_hierarchical_annotation_v2 is completed and its v2-haiku dataset asset is available.

steps.name: init-folders
steps.description: Create the mandatory task folder structure with assets/dataset placeholder.

steps.name: research-papers
steps.description: Review existing papers on hierarchical annotation, schema design, and judge-model agreement.

steps.name: research-internet
steps.description: Skipped: this task only re-applies an internal protocol and consumes prior task outputs.

steps.name: research-code
steps.description: Review t0009 v2 annotator/judge code, t0005 v1 dataset asset, and prior libraries to plan the re-run.

steps.name: planning
steps.description: Synthesize research into plan/plan.md covering reuse of t0009 prompts, sonnet rerun, and judge re-application.

steps.name: setup-machines
steps.description: Skipped: API-only task, no remote compute required.

steps.name: implementation
steps.description: Re-annotate the 115 v2 rows with claude-sonnet-4-6, run the haiku judge on the same 23-row sample, and persist the new dataset asset.

steps.name: creative-thinking
steps.description: Out-of-the-box analysis of schema vs model decomposition and follow-up directions.

steps.name: results
steps.description: Write results_summary.md, results_detailed.md, metrics.json with per-benchmark and aggregate accept-rate deltas plus charts.

steps.name: compare-literature
steps.description: Compare the schema vs model decomposition against published findings on schema effects in LLM annotation.

steps.name: suggestions
steps.description: Write suggestions.json with follow-ups (e.g. v3 schema iteration if schema component small, sonnet-default policy if model component dominates).

steps.name: reporting
steps.description: Run all verificators, capture sessions, mark task completed, push branch and prepare PR.
